title: crataegus tomentosa var. structilis
intro: Crataegus tomentosa var. structilis, also known as the Chinese hawthorn, is a variety of the Crataegus tomentosa plant, a type of deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiotonic effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, triterpenoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Chinese hawthorn include improved cardiovascular health, enhanced digestion, and potential anti-aging effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for heart health and digestive issues. Chinese hawthorn can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
